show this help message and exit
show this help message and exit
Plan name

Exit if the plan state doesn't change for this many iterations AND no new review.json has been written (default 5). Use --max-review-rework-cycles for the rework-loop limit — execute rework can span many iterations with state pinned at 'finalized', which is not a real stall.
Hard cap on loop iterations (default 200)

Cap on observed review→rework cycles before the driver bails (default 3). A rework cycle is counted each time review.json is rewritten while state appears stuck at 'finalized'. Mirrors execution.max_review_rework_cycles.
Abort automation after cumulative state history cost exceeds this USD cap. The check runs after each phase completes (default no cap).
Fresh execute retries to allow after Codex context-window exhaustion (default 2; 0 disables).
Fresh non-execute phase retries to allow after retryable external stream stalls or timeout-shaped network errors (default 1; 0 disables).
How many times to retry execute after the worker reports result=blocked (e.g. done tasks missing files_changed) before bailing with worker_blocked (default 1).

Consecutive `override add-note` failures to tolerate before escalating to `override force-proceed` (default 2). The driver synthesizes a note from the latest gate signals; the cap protects against loops where add-note itself keeps failing.

Consecutive execute failures (with no forward progress) before the driver escalates UP to the next more-capable distinct model in tier_models.execute and retries with a fresh session (default 2; 0 disables). It keeps climbing on further failures and caps at the most powerful distinct tier; once at that ceiling and still failing, control falls through to the state-stall manual_review halt. No-ops on runs without tier_models.execute.
What to do when the gate escalates (default force-proceed)
Seconds to sleep between phase transitions (default 1.0)
Seconds before a single phase subprocess (plan/prep/critique/gate/finalize/execute/review) is killed and treated as a failure (default 3600s). Stall detection still applies on top.
Seconds without stdout/stderr from a phase subprocess before auto kills it as idle (default 1800s; set 0 to disable).
Override the source-code working directory for subprocess workers (--add-dir / -C). Defaults to the current working directory.
Seconds before `megaplan status` / override subprocesses are killed (default 60s). These should always be quick; hitting this indicates serious trouble.
Write the final DriverOutcome JSON to this path atomically before stdout.

options.help: show this help message and exit
Path to the chain spec YAML (required at top-level or on subcommands)
Run the chain against this project directory instead of discovering from CWD.
Create a new git worktree at ~/Documents/.megaplan-worktrees/<name>/ on a new branch and run the whole chain inside it. Name must match ^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9._-]{0,63}$. Substitutes for --project-dir.
Base ref for the new worktree (default: current HEAD of the repo where `megaplan chain` was invoked). Only valid with --in-worktree.
With --in-worktree: remove an existing registered worktree/branch for this name before creating the new chain worktree.
With --in-worktree: fork from a clean base ref and leave any uncommitted state behind in the source repo (no carry).
With --in-worktree: explicitly opt into carrying uncommitted state from the source repo into the new worktree. Mutually exclusive with --clean-worktree.

Skip the automatic base-branch checkout and pull that runs before each milestone. Use this on developer checkouts where you do not want chain to stomp on the currently checked-out branch. Default: refresh enabled (preserves CI/orchestrator behavior).
Disable milestone branch creation, PR creation, commits, and pushes. Also enabled by MEGAPLAN_CHAIN_NO_PUSH=1; intended for local/no-network tests.
Drive at most one pending milestone, persist progress, then stop cleanly.
Require top-level anchors.north_star for this chain run (default unless spec opts out).
Opt out of the default top-level anchors.north_star requirement for this chain run.
Acknowledgement reason required when opting out and no top-level anchors.north_star is declared.

Use Hermes agent for all phases. Optional: specify default model (e.g. --hermes anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6)
Per-phase model override: --phase-model critique=hermes:openai/gpt-5
Named preset from profiles.toml; see 'megaplan config profiles list'.

Pick the premium vendor for tier-2-through-4 profile slots. Swaps claude:X <-> codex:X at the same effort tier; hermes specs untouched. Defaults to ~/.config/megaplan/config.toml [defaults].vendor (or 'claude'). Silently ignored when the active profile is vendor_locked = true.

Set author-phase thinking depth (plan / revise / loop_plan / tiebreaker_researcher / tiebreaker_challenger). Rewrites the effort suffix on claude:X / codex:X slots; critic and mechanical phases are not touched (asymmetry principle). hermes specs and profiles with no premium author slots are a silent no-op. Defaults to whatever depth the profile already sets (usually :low). Honored on vendor_locked profiles.

Override the critique+review pair (the critique == review invariant — same mind pre- and post-execution). 'kimi' swaps in Kimi (Fireworks-hosted kimi-k2p6) for both phases; 'cross' swaps to the other premium vendor relative to --vendor. Silently ignored on vendor_locked profiles.

Choose the provider for canonical DeepSeek v4-pro profile slots. Only 'direct' is supported; it uses hermes:deepseek:deepseek-v4-pro and DEEPSEEK_API_KEY. Defaults to 'direct'. Non-DeepSeek slots are untouched.

Override the source-code working directory passed to subprocess workers (--add-dir / -C). Defaults to the current working directory. Use this to force a specific path (e.g. a git worktree) regardless of where the plan was created.

Rewrites the effort suffix on claude:X / codex:X slots; critic and mechanical phases are not touched (asymmetry principle). hermes specs and profiles with no premium author slots are a silent no-op. Defaults to whatever depth the profile already sets (usually :low). Honored on vendor_locked profiles.

Override the critique+review pair (the critique == review invariant — same mind pre- and post-execution). 'kimi' swaps in Kimi (Fireworks-hosted kimi-k2p6) for both phases; 'cross' swaps to the other premium vendor relative to --vendor. Silently ignored on vendor_locked profiles.

Choose the provider for canonical DeepSeek v4-pro profile slots. Only 'direct' is supported; it uses hermes:deepseek:deepseek-v4-pro and DEEPSEEK_API_KEY. Defaults to 'direct'. Non-DeepSeek slots are untouched.

Override the source-code working directory passed to subprocess workers (--add-dir / -C). Defaults to the current working directory. Use this to force a specific path (e.g. a git worktree) regardless of where the plan was created.
Cap tier-routed execute lookup at N (1-5). A complexity above N routes as tier N; unset means no cap.
Execute a specific global batch number (1-indexed)

Reset any tasks persisted at status=blocked back to pending before computing batches. Use when re-running execute after resolving an external prerequisite that previously blocked a task. The auto-driver passes this on every fresh invocation.

Drop the per-batch tier-routed model by this many tiers for this dispatch (only meaningful when a profile defines tier_models.execute). The effective complexity used to look up the tier map is max(floor, batch_complexity - tier_drop), clamped at the premium floor (tier 3). The auto-driver raises this automatically after repeated worker stalls so an Opus-tier task that keeps stalling falls back to Sonnet before the run halts for manual review (default 0).

Plan name (required for edit/show)
edit: just scaffold the template (if missing) and print the path; do not open $EDITOR
workflow: re-run the AI rating pass even if feedback.md already has user fields. Overwrites ai_rating/ai_comment only; never touches user rating:/comment:.
search: substring match on plan profile (e.g. 'claude', 'apex')
search: substring match on plan project_dir / repo path
search: only show plans with Overall rating >= N
search: only show plans with Overall rating <= N
search: only show plans that have a rating for this stage
search: only show plans whose Overall comment is non-empty
search: scan all megaplan project roots on this machine, not just the current tree
search: emit raw JSON instead of a table

show this help message and exit
Create a new git worktree at ~/Documents/.megaplan-worktrees/<name>/ on a new branch and initialize the plan inside it. Name must match ^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9._-]{0,63}$. Substitutes for --project-dir.
Base ref for the new worktree (default: current HEAD of the repo where `megaplan init` was invoked). Only valid with --in-worktree.
With --in-worktree: fork from a clean base ref and leave any uncommitted state behind in the source repo (no carry).

With --in-worktree: explicitly opt into carrying uncommitted state from the source repo into the new worktree (this is the default already when the source is dirty; the flag exists for test/script clarity). Mutually exclusive with --clean-worktree.

Raise AdaptiveCritiqueDegradedError instead of silently falling back to static lenses when the adaptive critique evaluator fails. Recommended for production / CI / important runs. Has no effect when --adaptive-critique is off.
Pin every farmed-out adaptive-critique critic to this model (the Opus evaluator still selects lenses; no per-lens escalation).
Reject force-proceed while unabsorbed user notes exist; turn ESCALATE guidance into a hard human-required signal. Auto-on for --mode metaplan/doc.

Deliverable type: 'code' (source changes), 'doc' / 'metaplan' (design/spec artifact — 'metaplan' is an alias for 'doc'), or 'joke' (film scene script; requires --output), or 'creative' (creative work; requires --form and --output). Defaults to 'code' unless the idea strongly suggests a design document, in which case --mode must be passed explicitly.
Creative form to use with --mode creative.
Relative path where the prose artifact will be written. Required with --mode doc, --mode joke, or --mode creative; rejected with --mode code.
Declare the creative-work primary criterion (for example: 'weirdest coherent'). Valid only with --mode joke or --mode creative.
Relative path to a prior doc-mode artifact whose ## Settled Decisions section should be imported. Valid with --mode code, --mode doc, --mode joke, or --mode creative.
Snapshot a UTF-8 North Star anchor file into the new plan.
Read the idea text from a UTF-8 file instead of the positional CLI argument.
Immediately run the auto driver after initializing the plan.
Use Hermes agent for all phases. Optional: specify default model
Per-phase model override: --phase-model critique=hermes:openai/gpt-5
Named preset from profiles.toml; see 'megaplan config profiles list'.
Control the full-suite milestone-advance backstop: off disables it, shadow records only, enforce blocks on computed full-suite failures.

Pick the premium vendor for tier-2-through-4 profile slots. Swaps claude:X <-> codex:X at the same effort tier; hermes specs untouched. Defaults to ~/.config/megaplan/config.toml [defaults].vendor (or 'claude'). Silently ignored when the active profile is vendor_locked = true.

Set author-phase thinking depth (plan / revise / loop_plan / tiebreaker_researcher / tiebreaker_challenger). Rewrites the effort suffix on claude:X / codex:X slots; critic and mechanical phases are not touched (asymmetry principle). hermes specs and profiles with no premium author slots are a silent no-op. Defaults to whatever depth the profile already sets (usually :low). Honored on vendor_locked profiles.

Override the critique+review pair (the critique == review invariant — same mind pre- and post-execution). 'kimi' swaps in Kimi (Fireworks-hosted kimi-k2p6) for both phases; 'cross' swaps to the other premium vendor relative to --vendor. Silently ignored on vendor_locked profiles.

Choose the provider for canonical DeepSeek v4-pro profile slots. Only 'direct' is supported; it uses hermes:deepseek:deepseek-v4-pro and DEEPSEEK_API_KEY. Defaults to 'direct'. Non-DeepSeek slots are untouched.
Cap tier-routed execute lookup at N (1-5). A complexity above N routes as tier N; unset means no cap.

Force the visible prep phase into the workflow regardless of --robustness. By default, prep only runs at --robustness thorough|extreme; this flag adds prep to full / light / bare so the planner can do explicit research before committing to a plan. Useful for unfamiliar libraries, novel external APIs, research-heavy briefs, or ambiguous requirements. Redundant on --robustness thorough|extreme (no-op).

Force the visible feedback phase into the workflow regardless of --robustness. By default no feedback step runs; this flag adds a feedback step between review and done that scaffolds feedback.md (a per-stage ratings template) for the user to fill in afterward. Runs non-interactively under megaplan auto — never blocks on human input.

Disable the prep clarify gate so the prep phase never halts at awaiting_human_verify to ask a clarifying question (it proceeds with its best understanding instead). Only effective when prep runs. Used by chain milestones with prep_clarify: false for unattended runs.

Steering text shown to the prep worker as 'User direction for prep'. Use to point prep at specific files, subsystems, or questions to explore (e.g. 'focus on the worker shutdown path; ignore CLI plumbing'). Only effective when prep runs (robustness thorough|extreme, or --with-prep).
Load plan idea from Arnold epic via DBStore (read-only; --backend db not required for read path)

Override the source-code working directory passed to subprocess workers (--add-dir / -C). Defaults to the current working directory. Use this to force a specific path (e.g. a git worktree) regardless of where the plan was created.

Set or replace the prep direction (state.config.prep_direction) before the prep worker runs. Same semantics as `init --prep-direction`, but applied at prep time so you can steer prep without re-initializing.

Pick the premium vendor for tier-2-through-4 profile slots. Swaps claude:X <-> codex:X at the same effort tier; hermes specs untouched. Defaults to ~/.config/megaplan/config.toml [defaults].vendor (or 'claude'). Silently ignored when the active profile is vendor_locked = true.

Set author-phase thinking depth (plan / revise / loop_plan / tiebreaker_researcher / tiebreaker_challenger). Rewrites the effort suffix on claude:X / codex:X slots; critic and mechanical phases are not touched (asymmetry principle). hermes specs and profiles with no premium author slots are a silent no-op. Defaults to whatever depth the profile already sets (usually :low). Honored on vendor_locked profiles.
